verses.text: Moab rebels, and Ahaziah, injured in a fall, sends to consult Baal-zebub, god of Ekron — as if there were no God in Israel. Elijah intercepts the messengers with that very rebuke. To seek answers anywhere but from the living God is to insult the One who is near.

Moab rebels, and Ahaziah, injured in a fall, sends to consult Baal-zebub, god of Ekron — as if there were no God in Israel. Elijah intercepts the messengers with that very rebuke. To seek answers anywhere but from the living God is to insult the One who is near.

Moab rebels, and Ahaziah, injured in a fall, sends to consult Baal-zebub, god of Ekron — as if there were no God in Israel. Elijah intercepts the messengers with that very rebuke. To seek answers anywhere but from the living God is to insult the One who is near.

Moab rebels, and Ahaziah, injured in a fall, sends to consult Baal-zebub, god of Ekron — as if there were no God in Israel. Elijah intercepts the messengers with that very rebuke. To seek answers anywhere but from the living God is to insult the One who is near.

The messengers return early, describing the hairy man in a leather girdle, and Ahaziah knows it is Elijah. The king recognizes the prophet but not the warning. Men can identify God's truth and still refuse to heed it; knowledge without obedience saves no one.

The messengers return early, describing the hairy man in a leather girdle, and Ahaziah knows it is Elijah. The king recognizes the prophet but not the warning. Men can identify God's truth and still refuse to heed it; knowledge without obedience saves no one.

Twice Ahaziah sends a captain with fifty to seize Elijah, each demanding he come down, and twice fire from heaven consumes them. God will not be summoned or arrested by an arrogant king. To approach the holy with contempt is to court the very judgment one mocks.

Twice Ahaziah sends a captain with fifty to seize Elijah, each demanding he come down, and twice fire from heaven consumes them. God will not be summoned or arrested by an arrogant king. To approach the holy with contempt is to court the very judgment one mocks.

The third captain falls on his knees and begs for his life and his men's, and Elijah, told by God it is safe, goes with him. Humility finds mercy where arrogance found fire. The same errand, approached with reverence instead of pride, ends in life, not death.

The third captain falls on his knees and begs for his life and his men's, and Elijah, told by God it is safe, goes with him. Humility finds mercy where arrogance found fire. The same errand, approached with reverence instead of pride, ends in life, not death.

The third captain falls on his knees and begs for his life and his men's, and Elijah, told by God it is safe, goes with him. Humility finds mercy where arrogance found fire. The same errand, approached with reverence instead of pride, ends in life, not death.

Elijah tells Ahaziah to his face: because you sought Baal-zebub and not God, you shall surely die — and he does. The word stands; the king never rises from his bed. Turning from God in the hour of need does not avert disaster but seals it.
